item.title: Specific heat and density anomaly in the Hubbard model
item.description:
Author(s): M. A. Habitzreuter, Willdauany C. de Freitas Silva, Eduardo O. Rizzatti, Thereza Paiva, and Marcia C. Barbosa

Understanding thermal properties of materials is fundamental to technological applications and to discovering new phenomena. In particular, advances in experimental techniques such as cold-atom measurements allow the simulation of paradigmatic Hamiltonians with great control over model parameters, s…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165111] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): M. A. Habitzreuter, Willdauany C. de Freitas Silva, Eduardo O. Rizzatti, Thereza Paiva, and Marcia C. Barbosa

Understanding thermal properties of materials is fundamental to technological applications and to discovering new phenomena. In particular, advances in experimental techniques such as cold-atom measurements allow the simulation of paradigmatic Hamiltonians with great control over model parameters, s…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165111] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026

item.title: Thermoelectric evidence of the electronic structure changes from the charge density wave transition in FeGe
item.description:
Author(s): Kaila Jenkins, Yuan Zhu, Dechen Zhang, Guoxin Zheng, Kuan-Wen Chen, Aaron Chan, Sijie Xu, Mason L. Klemm, Bin Gao, Ming Yi, Pengcheng Dai, and Lu Li

Kagome metals provide a material platform for probing new correlated quantum phenomena due to the naturally incorporated linear dispersions, flat bands, and Van Hove singularities in their electronic structures. Among these quantum phenomena is the charge density wave (CDW), or the distortion of the…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165112] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Kaila Jenkins, Yuan Zhu, Dechen Zhang, Guoxin Zheng, Kuan-Wen Chen, Aaron Chan, Sijie Xu, Mason L. Klemm, Bin Gao, Ming Yi, Pengcheng Dai, and Lu Li

Kagome metals provide a material platform for probing new correlated quantum phenomena due to the naturally incorporated linear dispersions, flat bands, and Van Hove singularities in their electronic structures. Among these quantum phenomena is the charge density wave (CDW), or the distortion of the…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165112] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026

item.title: Pairing around a single Dirac point: A unifying view of Kohn-Luttinger superconductivity in Chern bands, quarter metals, and topological surface states
item.description:
Author(s): Omid Tavakol and Thomas Scaffidi

Superconductivity of a single two-dimensional Dirac fermion offers a natural route to topological superconductivity. While usually considered extrinsic—arising from proximity to a conventional superconductor—we investigate when a doped Dirac cone can spontaneously develop superconductivity from a sh…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144502] Published Tue Apr 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Omid Tavakol and Thomas Scaffidi

Superconductivity of a single two-dimensional Dirac fermion offers a natural route to topological superconductivity. While usually considered extrinsic—arising from proximity to a conventional superconductor—we investigate when a doped Dirac cone can spontaneously develop superconductivity from a sh…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144502] Published Tue Apr 07, 2026
item.dc:title: Pairing around a single Dirac point: A unifying view of Kohn-Luttinger superconductivity in Chern bands, quarter metals, and topological surface states
item.dc:creator: Omid Tavakol and Thomas Scaffidi
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: Spin-orbit driven topological phases in kagome materials
item.description:
Author(s): Chi Wu and Tiantian Zhang

Here, the authors investigate spin-orbit coupling (SOC) effects in kagome-type IAMX materials through a combination of theoretical modeling and first-principles calculations (IA = alkali metal, M = rare earth metal, and X = carbon group element). By developing a minimal four-band spinful model, the study captures SOC-induced topological phase transitions, illustrating the continuous evolution of phase diagrams and topological surface states. The model is supported by systematic DFT calculations across different materials, bridging theoretical models with real-world materials. This research serves as a valuable guide for leveraging IAMX materials in multifunctional device applications.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155115] Published Tue Apr 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chi Wu and Tiantian Zhang

Here, the authors investigate spin-orbit coupling (SOC) effects in kagome-type IAMX materials through a combination of theoretical modeling and first-principles calculations (IA = alkali metal, M = rare earth metal, and X = carbon group element). By developing a minimal four-band spinful model, the study captures SOC-induced topological phase transitions, illustrating the continuous evolution of phase diagrams and topological surface states. The model is supported by systematic DFT calculations across different materials, bridging theoretical models with real-world materials. This research serves as a valuable guide for leveraging IAMX materials in multifunctional device applications.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155115] Published Tue Apr 07, 2026
item.dc:title: Spin-orbit driven topological phases in kagome materials
item.dc:creator: Chi Wu and Tiantian Zhang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Holon metal, charge density wave metal, and chiral superconductor from doping a fractional Chern insulator and an ${\mathrm{SU}(3)}_{1}$ chiral spin liquid
item.description:
Author(s): Ya-Hui Zhang

Recent experiments have observed superconductivity proximate to the $ν=−2/3$ fractional quantum anomalous Hall (FQAH) insulator in twisted ${\mathrm{MoTe}}_{2}$. A critical open question is whether the underlying normal state is a Fermi liquid with a large Fermi surface or a strongly correlated meta…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155110] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ya-Hui Zhang

Recent experiments have observed superconductivity proximate to the ν=−2/3 fractional quantum anomalous Hall (FQAH) insulator in twisted MoTe2. A critical open question is whether the underlying normal state is a Fermi liquid with a large Fermi surface or a strongly correlated metal with low carrier…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155110] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Holon metal, charge density wave metal, and chiral superconductor from doping a fractional Chern insulator and an ${\mathrm{SU}(3)}_{1}$ chiral spin liquid

item.title: Dynamical correlations and Mott localization in a paramagnetic ${\mathrm{VI}}_{3}$ bulk crystal
item.description:
Author(s): Luis Craco and Sabrina S. Carara

We perform a comprehensive study on the correlated electronic structure reconstruction of paramagnetic ${\mathrm{VI}}_{3}$ crystal in its rhombohedral and monoclinic structural phases. Using density-functional plus dynamical mean-field theory ($\mathrm{DFT}+\mathrm{DMFT}$) calculations, we explicitl…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155113] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Luis Craco and Sabrina S. Carara

We perform a comprehensive study on the correlated electronic structure reconstruction of paramagnetic VI3 crystal in its rhombohedral and monoclinic structural phases. Using density-functional plus dynamical mean-field theory (DFT+DMFT) calculations, we explicitly demonstrate the importance of loca…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155113] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Dynamical correlations and Mott localization in a paramagnetic ${\mathrm{VI}}_{3}$ bulk crystal

item.title: Electron energy loss function in a graphene-hBN-graphene nanostructure
item.description:
Author(s): Ana Kalinić, Vito Despoja, Ivan Radović, Lazar Karbunar, and Zoran L. Mišković

The aim of this study is to theoretically investigate the loss function of ${\mathrm{layer}}_{1}$-hBN-${\mathrm{layer}}_{2}$ nanostructures, where layers 1 and 2 may be doped graphene, pristine graphene, or vacuum. This was achieved by comparing results obtained from the ab initio and massless Dirac…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155408] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ana Kalinić, Vito Despoja, Ivan Radović, Lazar Karbunar, and Zoran L. Mišković

The aim of this study is to theoretically investigate the loss function of layer1-hBN-layer2 nanostructures, where layers 1 and 2 may be doped graphene, pristine graphene, or vacuum. This was achieved by comparing results obtained from the ab initio and massless Dirac fermion approaches and by exami…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155408] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Electron energy loss function in a graphene-hBN-graphene nanostructure

item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Interplay between non-Fermi liquid and non-Hermiticity: A multimethod study of non-Hermitian multichannel Kondo model
item.description:
Author(s): Wei-Zhu Yi, Yun Chen, Jun-Jun Pang, Hong Chen, Baigeng Wang, and Rui Wang

The authors construct here an open system setup that realizes a non-Hermitian multichannel Kondo problem. By applying multiple nonperturbative analytic and numerical methods, they identify three phases enriched by non-Hermiticity. The computed impurity entropy exceeds the free local moment value of ln2, indicating the emergence of a Yu–Shiba–Rusinov-like impurity state. The corresponding Kondo conductance exhibits an anomalous temperature scaling induced by non-Hermiticity, pointing to transport phenomena distinct from those in conventional Hermitian Kondo systems.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165110] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Wei-Zhu Yi, Yun Chen, Jun-Jun Pang, Hong Chen, Baigeng Wang, and Rui Wang

The authors construct here an open system setup that realizes a non-Hermitian multichannel Kondo problem. By applying multiple nonperturbative analytic and numerical methods, they identify three phases enriched by non-Hermiticity. The computed impurity entropy exceeds the free local moment value of ln2, indicating the emergence of a Yu–Shiba–Rusinov-like impurity state. The corresponding Kondo conductance exhibits an anomalous temperature scaling induced by non-Hermiticity, pointing to transport phenomena distinct from those in conventional Hermitian Kondo systems.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165110] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Interplay between non-Fermi liquid and non-Hermiticity: A multimethod study of non-Hermitian multichannel Kondo model
item.dc:creator: Wei-Zhu Yi, Yun Chen, Jun-Jun Pang, Hong Chen, Baigeng Wang, and Rui Wang

item.title: Strain- and doping-tunable optical resonance in Kekulé-Y graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Yawar Mohammadi

We investigate the optical response of Kekulé-Y graphene under uniaxial strain and carrier doping. Using a low-energy effective Hamiltonian, we show that strain reshapes the low-energy electronic structure of the Kekulé-Y phase and induces van Hove singularities at energies well below those of prist…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165406] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yawar Mohammadi

We investigate the optical response of Kekulé-Y graphene under uniaxial strain and carrier doping. Using a low-energy effective Hamiltonian, we show that strain reshapes the low-energy electronic structure of the Kekulé-Y phase and induces van Hove singularities at energies well below those of prist…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165406] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Strain- and doping-tunable optical resonance in Kekulé-Y graphene
item.dc:creator: Yawar Mohammadi

item.title: Quantum phase diagram of the spin-$\frac{1}{2}$ Heisenberg antiferromagnet on the square-kagome lattice: A tensor network study
item.description:
Author(s): Saeed S. Jahromi and Yasir Iqbal

We study the ground-state phase diagram of the spin-$1/2$ antiferromagnetic Heisenberg model on the square-kagome lattice using infinite projected entangled-pair states. By systematically varying the ratio of exchange couplings on triangular and square plaquettes, we establish a complete quantum pha…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L140402] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Saeed S. Jahromi and Yasir Iqbal

We study the ground-state phase diagram of the spin-1/2 antiferromagnetic Heisenberg model on the square-kagome lattice using infinite projected entangled-pair states. By systematically varying the ratio of exchange couplings on triangular and square plaquettes, we establish a complete quantum phase…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L140402] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum phase diagram of the spin-$\frac{1}{2}$ Heisenberg antiferromagnet on the square-kagome lattice: A tensor network study

item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: High-temperature quantum anomalous Hall effect in buckled honeycomb antiferromagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Mohsen Hafez-Torbati and Götz S. Uhrig

We propose Néel antiferromagnetic (AF) Mott insulators with a buckled honeycomb structure as potential candidates to host a high-temperature AF Chern insulator (AFCI). Using a generalized Kondo lattice model, we show that the staggered potential induced by a perpendicular electric field due to the b…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L161105] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mohsen Hafez-Torbati and Götz S. Uhrig

We propose Néel antiferromagnetic (AF) Mott insulators with a buckled honeycomb structure as potential candidates to host a high-temperature AF Chern insulator (AFCI). Using a generalized Kondo lattice model, we show that the staggered potential induced by a perpendicular electric field due to the b…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L161105] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.dc:title: High-temperature quantum anomalous Hall effect in buckled honeycomb antiferromagnets

item.title: Spin-mixed edge states and their general expressions for conductance and local bond current in graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Xiao-Long Lü, Pei-Hao Fu, Lie-Run Tian, Xiang-Long Yu, and Jun-Feng Liu

Generating spin-mixed edge states with hybridized spin-up and spin-down components and clarifying their transport mechanisms are critical to topological spintronic and valleytronic devices. In this work, we propose two types of spatially modulated Haldane-Rashba models that incorporate spatial varia…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155403] Published Fri Apr 03, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xiao-Long Lü, Pei-Hao Fu, Lie-Run Tian, Xiang-Long Yu, and Jun-Feng Liu

Generating spin-mixed edge states with hybridized spin-up and spin-down components and clarifying their transport mechanisms are critical to topological spintronic and valleytronic devices. In this work, we propose two types of spatially modulated Haldane-Rashba models that incorporate spatial varia…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155403] Published Fri Apr 03, 2026

item.title: Kagome bands and magnetism in $\mathrm{MoT}{\mathrm{e}}_{2−x}$ kagome monolayers
item.description:
Author(s): Jiaqi Dai, Zhongqin Zhang, Zemin Pan, Cong Wang, Chendong Zhang, Zhihai Cheng, and Wei Ji

Kagome lattices facilitate various quantum phases, yet in bulk materials, their kagome flat bands often interact with bulk bands, suppressing kagome electronic characteristics for hosting these phases. Here, we use density functional theory calculations to predict the geometric and electronic struct…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155405] Published Fri Apr 03, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jiaqi Dai, Zhongqin Zhang, Zemin Pan, Cong Wang, Chendong Zhang, Zhihai Cheng, and Wei Ji

Kagome lattices facilitate various quantum phases, yet in bulk materials, their kagome flat bands often interact with bulk bands, suppressing kagome electronic characteristics for hosting these phases. Here, we use density functional theory calculations to predict the geometric and electronic struct…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155405] Published Fri Apr 03, 2026

item.title: Terahertz enhancement in NiO/Fe/Pt heterostructures driven by laser-induced magneto-ionic interfacial reconstruction
item.description:
Author(s): Jia Xu, Xianguo Jiang, Shaohua Zhang, Yaxuan Jin, Lei Hao, Ning Yang, Yizheng Wu, Feng Xu, Wendeng Huang, Hao Meng, Johan Åkerman, Yan Zhou, and Chao Zhou

Antiferromagnetic oxide materials are emerging as key ingredients in spintronic devices and ultrafast terahertz (THz) emitters due to their distinct spin dynamics and vanishing net magnetization. Here, we report an irreversible, time-dependent enhancement of THz emission from exchange-coupled NiO/Fe…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144404] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jia Xu, Xianguo Jiang, Shaohua Zhang, Yaxuan Jin, Lei Hao, Ning Yang, Yizheng Wu, Feng Xu, Wendeng Huang, Hao Meng, Johan Åkerman, Yan Zhou, and Chao Zhou

Antiferromagnetic oxide materials are emerging as key ingredients in spintronic devices and ultrafast terahertz (THz) emitters due to their distinct spin dynamics and vanishing net magnetization. Here, we report an irreversible, time-dependent enhancement of THz emission from exchange-coupled NiO/Fe…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144404] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Terahertz enhancement in NiO/Fe/Pt heterostructures driven by laser-induced magneto-ionic interfacial reconstruction

item.title: Paramagnetic state of the kagome Kondo lattice compound ${\mathrm{YbV}}_{6}{\mathrm{Sn}}_{6}$: A $^{51}\mathrm{V}$ nuclear magnetic resonance study
item.description:
Author(s): S. Park, H. Sakai, S. Hosoi, S. M. Thomas, S. Kambe, Y. Tokunaga, A. P. Dioguardi, J. D. Thompson, F. Ronning, M. Kimata, T. Furukawa, T. Sasaki, E. D. Bauer, and M. Hirata

The Kondo lattice compound ${\mathrm{YbV}}_{6}{\mathrm{Sn}}_{6}$ has recently been discovered to exhibit antiferromagnetic order at ${T}_{\mathrm{N}}≈0.4\phantom{\rule{0.28em}{0ex}}\mathrm{K}$ and features a layered structure that combines a triangular lattice network of ${\mathrm{Yb}}^{3+}$ ions wi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155107] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): S. Park, H. Sakai, S. Hosoi, S. M. Thomas, S. Kambe, Y. Tokunaga, A. P. Dioguardi, J. D. Thompson, F. Ronning, M. Kimata, T. Furukawa, T. Sasaki, E. D. Bauer, and M. Hirata

The Kondo lattice compound YbV6Sn6 has recently been discovered to exhibit antiferromagnetic order at TN≈0.4K and features a layered structure that combines a triangular lattice network of Yb3+ ions with a vanadium-based kagome network. We report a V51 nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) study of the p…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155107] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Paramagnetic state of the kagome Kondo lattice compound ${\mathrm{YbV}}_{6}{\mathrm{Sn}}_{6}$: A $^{51}\mathrm{V}$ nuclear magnetic resonance study

item.title: Microscopic origin of period-four stripe charge density wave in the kagome metal ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$
item.description:
Author(s): Yuma Murata, Rina Tazai, Youichi Yamakawa, Seiichiro Onari, and Hiroshi Kontani

The interplay between unconventional density waves and exotic superconductivity has attracted growing interest. Kagome superconductors $A{\mathrm{V}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ ($A=\mathrm{K},\mathrm{Rb},\mathrm{Cs}$) offer a platform for studying quantum phase transitions and the resulting symmetry brea…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155108] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yuma Murata, Rina Tazai, Youichi Yamakawa, Seiichiro Onari, and Hiroshi Kontani

The interplay between unconventional density waves and exotic superconductivity has attracted growing interest. Kagome superconductors AV3Sb5 (A=K,Rb,Cs) offer a platform for studying quantum phase transitions and the resulting symmetry breaking. Among these quantum phases, the 4a0 stripe charge-den…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155108] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Microscopic origin of period-four stripe charge density wave in the kagome metal ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$
item.dc:creator: Yuma Murata, Rina Tazai, Youichi Yamakawa, Seiichiro Onari, and Hiroshi Kontani

item.title: Flux-driven charge and spin transport in a dimerized Hubbard ring with Fibonacci modulation
item.description:
Author(s): Souvik Roy, Soumya Ranjan Padhi, and Tapan Mishra

We study quantum transport in a one-dimensional Hubbard ring with dimerized nearest-neighbor hoppings and a Fibonacci-modulated on-site potential. For a noninteracting case our analysis reveals that at half filling, the charge current along with the Drude weight decreases with increasing on-site pot…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155401] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Souvik Roy, Soumya Ranjan Padhi, and Tapan Mishra

We study quantum transport in a one-dimensional Hubbard ring with dimerized nearest-neighbor hoppings and a Fibonacci-modulated on-site potential. For a noninteracting case our analysis reveals that at half filling, the charge current along with the Drude weight decreases with increasing on-site pot…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155401] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Flux-driven charge and spin transport in a dimerized Hubbard ring with Fibonacci modulation
item.dc:creator: Souvik Roy, Soumya Ranjan Padhi, and Tapan Mishra

item.title: Spin-fluctuation-mediated chiral $d+i{d}^{′}$-wave superconductivity in the $α$–${\mathcal{T}}_{3}$ lattice with an incipient flat band
item.description:
Author(s): Masataka Kakoi and Kazuhiko Kuroki

We study anisotropic superconductivity in the nearly quarter-filled $α$–${\mathcal{T}}_{3}$ lattice. We analyze an extended Hubbard model with off-site attractive interactions within the mean-field framework and find two distinct chiral $d+i{d}^{′}$-wave superconducting phases characterized by diffe…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165104] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Masataka Kakoi and Kazuhiko Kuroki

We study anisotropic superconductivity in the nearly quarter-filled α–T3 lattice. We analyze an extended Hubbard model with off-site attractive interactions within the mean-field framework and find two distinct chiral d+id′-wave superconducting phases characterized by different Chern numbers. We fur…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165104] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Spin-fluctuation-mediated chiral $d+i{d}^{′}$-wave superconductivity in the $α$–${\mathcal{T}}_{3}$ lattice with an incipient flat band
item.dc:creator: Masataka Kakoi and Kazuhiko Kuroki
